title: DOCKER Container Port Mapping Validation Bug Fixer

statement.markdown: Write a function that validates the port mappings in a Docker Compose file. The function should take a string representing the Docker Compose file and return `True` if the port mappings are valid and `False` otherwise. Port mappings are valid if each port is a positive integer and no two ports are mapped to the same host port.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the Docker Compose file.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the port mappings are valid.
statement.notes: The Docker Compose file is in JSON format.

examples.explanation: Port 8080 is mapped to two different host ports
Port 'a' is not a positive integer

hints.text: First hint text: Parse the Docker Compose file and extract the port mappings.
Second hint text: Validate each port to ensure it is a positive integer.
Third hint text: Check if any two ports are mapped to the same host port.
